texto: If I have one piece of advice to give you all about what to search for in your quest for What Next, it's to find where the line blurs between hard work and happiness for you. To find that thing where working hard at it makes you happy, and where you're happy to work hard at it.
explicacao: Citação de Tom Fletcher sobre temas ligados a felicidade. “If I have one piece of advice to give you all about what to search for in your quest for What Nex…” articula felicidade, 2017, achievement em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e trabalho.

seo.titleSeo: "If I have one piece of advice to give you all about …" — Tom Fletcher | Metamensagem
seo.descriptionSeo: Citação de Tom Fletcher sobre temas ligados a felicidade. “If I have one piece of advice to give you all about what to search for in your quest for What Nex…”…

frase_original: If I have one piece of advice to give you all about what to search for in your quest for What Next, it's to find where the line blurs between hard work and happiness for you. To find that thing where working hard at it makes you happy, and where you're happy to work hard at it.

texto: I've never had a moment when I thought: "Tom, you've made it!", and I don't think I want to, because that feels like you've reached the end; that you've crossed the finish line. But to me there is no end. An achievement is not a finish line; it's a checkpoint on a far greater journey. It's a moment to pause, to take a breath and look back and enjoy what you've experienced and be grateful for it, but then to turn around and look towards the next checkpoint, the next achievement.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Tom Fletcher: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I've never had a moment when I thought: "Tom, you've made it!", and I don't think I want to, beca…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, 2017, achievement pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mudanca e viagem.

frase_original: I've never had a moment when I thought: "Tom, you've made it!", and I don't think I want to, because that feels like you've reached the end; that you've crossed the finish line. But to me there is no end. An achievement is not a finish line; it's a checkpoint on a far greater journey. It's a moment to pause, to take a breath and look back and enjoy what you've experienced and be grateful for it, but then to turn around and look towards the next checkpoint, the next achievement.
